message:
fix(core): three-valued BETWEEN semantics and collation snapshot in GROUP BY distinct aggregates

Two SQL evaluation correctness fixes:

Three-valued BETWEEN (connection.rs):
- BETWEEN now implements proper three-valued logic matching SQLite:
  `x BETWEEN y AND z` ≡ `x >= y AND x <= z` with NULL propagation
  through AND. Critically, FALSE AND NULL = FALSE (short-circuit),
  so `5 BETWEEN 10 AND NULL` is FALSE, not NULL. The previous
  implementation returned NULL whenever any operand was NULL.

Collation snapshot in GROUP BY (connection.rs):
- GROUP BY key comparison and DISTINCT aggregate deduplication now
  use the pre-snapshotted collation registry (coll_snap) instead of
  locking self.collation_registry per comparison. This is consistent
  with the sort path fixed in the prior commit and eliminates
  O(N) lock acquisitions during group merging and distinct filtering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
